# Frozen baseline release bundle (in-wheel copy)

This directory is the **in-wheel** copy of the frozen baselines bundle.
It ships inside `pip install bucket-brigade` and is the first location
checked by `bucket_brigade.baselines.release.resolve_artifact_path`.

**Status (post-#371)**: archetype + NE artifacts can be regenerated by
the freeze script (see "How operators populate this" below). PPO
checkpoints land here once issue #384 produces them.

## Contents layout

```
local/
    manifest.json           # see bucket_brigade/baselines/release/manifest.py
    archetypes/
        hero.json
        firefighter.json
        free_rider.json
        coordinator.json
        liar.json
    nash/
        minimal_specialization-v1.json   # best converged NE from heterogeneous sweep
        rest_trap-v1.json
        phase_diagram/
            b0.50_k0.50_c0.50.json       # one per phase-diagram preview cell
            ...
    ppo/                                 # populated by #384
        minimal_specialization-v1.pt
        ...
```

The `manifest.json` is the single source of truth: every file in this
directory must be referenced by exactly one `ArtifactEntry`.

Artifacts use **JSON** for archetypes and Nash vectors (small 10-dim
float vectors, language-agnostic, diffable in code review) and `.pt`
torch state dicts for PPO checkpoints (binary, large).

## How operators populate this

To (re)generate the archetype and Nash slices from the canonical
in-repo sources:

```bash
# From the repo root:
python -m bucket_brigade.baselines.release.freeze

# Preview without writing (also reports files a real run would refuse
# to delete):
python -m bucket_brigade.baselines.release.freeze --dry-run
```

Sources read:

- Archetypes: `bucket_brigade/agents/archetypes.py`
- Heterogeneous NE: `experiments/nash/heterogeneous/<scenario>/results.json`
- Phase-diagram cells: `experiments/nash/phase_diagram/preview/*/cells/*/results.json`

**Unreproducible-files guard (#473)**: the freeze refuses to run if the
bundle contains artifact files it cannot regenerate from the sources
above, instead of silently deleting them. The 29 full-grid
phase-diagram genomes added by #420 are exactly such files — their
per-cell solver outputs were never committed (only the aggregated
summary at `experiments/nash/phase_diagram/results.json`, which has no
genome vectors), so the tracked bytes here are the canonical record.
Pass `--force` only after reviewing the refusal list; a forced freeze
deletes those files permanently. Manifest changes for unreproducible
artifacts go through a `load_manifest` -> `ArtifactEntry` ->
`save_manifest` round-trip instead (see #470/#472/#473).

To add PPO checkpoints (after #384 lands), stage the trained
checkpoints under
`experiments/p3_specialization/baselines/<scenario>/checkpoint.pt` and
re-run the freeze script — it copies any present checkpoints into the
bundle and refreshes the manifest entries.

To validate / upload after re-freezing:

1. `git diff bucket_brigade/baselines/release/local/` — sanity-check
   what changed.
2. `python -m scripts.release.upload_to_hf --source-dir bucket_brigade/baselines/release/local --dry-run`
   to validate the manifest references every file on disk.
3. Commit and build a wheel (`uv build`).
4. (Optional) Mirror to HuggingFace with `--confirm` (operator step).
